title: Service Line Replacement Map, EPA 2027 Full-Replacement Mandate, and Homeowner Coordination
summary: Commissioner presents 2,900-home replacement map targeting homes where public-side was previously replaced (2005-2007) but private-side lead remains. EPA mandate effective November 2027 requires full service line replacement (main to meter); city already proactively doing full replacements. Commissioner explains public vs private side: city owns main-to-curb-stop, homeowner owns curb-stop-to-meter. Even after replacement, orthophosphate treatment continues because internal plumbing (solder, faucets) may contain lead. 800 full replacements completed in pump station project areas: Eastwood, Mosley, Forest Hill, Sunnycrest, University area -- commissioner kept going street-by-street beyond project boundaries. Homeowner coordination required for basement access; some residents refuse. Free service for renters and owners alike. Ehrenreich commends crew professionalism observed on west and east sides. Water from Skaneateles Lake is lead-free at source -- 100+ year infrastructure is steel, cast iron, ductile iron (even hollowed Adirondack logs) but does not introduce lead. Lead enters only at individual home service line connections.

political_signal.description: Commissioner championing proactive full replacement over minimum EPA public-side mandate; Ehrenreich commends crew professionalism

processing_notes: Homeowner access refusal is a documented barrier -- city logs attempts and can bypass after repeated refusals
Renter vs owner coordination question raised but not resolved ('we haven't came to that yet')
Infrastructure note: Skaneateles Lake water system includes hollowed Adirondack logs still in service
Segment 2 of 7 from Public Works and Infrastructure Committee Meeting, January 20th 2026
Potential duplicate detected: overlaps with CTX-COUNCIL-2026-00052 -- linked, not skipped
2026-03-21: Linked to CLUSTER-LEAD-REMEDIATION-2026Q1 (7 records from Jan 20 Public Works Committee lead water discussion).
